description_long: This is a short wooden fence section composed of seven evenly spaced vertical slats connected by two horizontal rails at the top and bottom. The wood appears to be smooth and uniformly colored, with a light brown finish typical of treated pine or cedar. The ends of the horizontal rails extend slightly beyond the outermost slats, giving the fence a framed appearance. There are no visible decorative elements, carvings, or embellishments, and the top edge of the fence is flat and level. The posts and rails are rectangular in cross-section, and the construction suggests it is a lightweight, movable barrier suitable for gardens, parks, or temporary urban demarcation. The overall design is simple and functional, prioritizing utility over ornamentation.
description: A simple, light brown wooden fence section with seven vertical slats and two horizontal rails, featuring a flat top and no decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing all seven vertical slats and the two horizontal rails, with the full width and structure visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence, with only the edge of the rails and slats visible.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the same arrangement of slats and rails.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow edge of the fence with minimal detail.
